data.body: Done definition update: Migration event timestamp policy — each FILE_IMPACT event emitted by the migration MUST use the ORIGINAL ticket.created_at as its event timestamp (not migration wall-clock time). This preserves chronology so (a) the reducer latest-wins tiebreaker with any future FILE_IMPACT events correctly picks the user-written value over the migration backfill, and (b) sprint-drift-check.sh (story 2998-a1ba) sees the file_impact as pre-existing relative to children created_at and does not spuriously classify the migration itself as drift. The migration stamp file records {version: file-impact-v1, migrated_at, tickets_processed, tickets_skipped} so re-runs are idempotent by stamp AND by per-ticket last-event check. [adversarial-review, blue-team accepted]
